creator: ohauer@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2011-10-03 18:20, Kim Scarborough wrote:
> The following reply was made to PR ports/159376; it has been noted by GNATS.
>
> From: Kim Scarborough <sluggo@unknown.nu>
> To: bug-followup@FreeBSD.org, vsityz@gmail.com
> Cc:
> Subject: Re: ports/159376: security/nmap 5.59BETA1 not working
> Date: Mon, 03 Oct 2011 10:58:23 -0500
>
> Actually, I was encountering the same problem, but I got it to work by
> building it against the pcap from net/libpcap rather than nmap's
> internal pcap. So maybe if IPV6 is disabled, there could be some logic
> in the Makefile to require net/libpcap as a prerequisite and add
> "--with-libpcap=$PREFIX" to CONFIGURE_ARGS.
>

This sounds strange since nmap is using libpcap from the OS not
the included version, you can check this with ldd.

I've created a update patch to nmap-5.61TEST2 and have included your
founding (only build tested since I have no system without IPv6)

http://people.freebsd.org/~ohauer/diffs/nmap-5.61TEST2.diff

Please note if the option WITHOUT_INET6 was specified in /etc/src.conf
it has to be defined during nmap configure/build time as command parameter
( make -DWITHOUT_INET6 ).

If you can verify this fix the IPv4 only issue I will update the port
with this workaround.

-- 
Regards,
olli
